# nominee

Fresh tokens, human approval, and an audit trail for agents that act on your users' behalf — framework-neutral, no SaaS.

nominee is a framework-agnostic TypeScript library that keeps a delegated third-party OAuth token fresh at the moment of every AI agent tool call — with optional human-in-the-loop approval and a full audit trail. It is provider-neutral: Auth0 is one optional strategy, not a requirement.

Layers: your agent/framework (Vercel AI SDK, Eve, Cloudflare Agents, standalone) → nominee (freshness, approval, audit, delegation) → your vault/store (env, DB, OAuth2, Auth0 Token Vault, Supabase).

npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/nominee
GitHub: https://github.com/bharath31/nominee
Docs: https://nominee.dev/docs/
License: MIT
Current version: 2.0.2

---

## Key concepts

- **Strategy**: The token broker, and the seam that lets you swap vaults without rewriting your agent. `tokens()` wraps any async function. `OAuth2({ connections })` is the zero-dep refresh-token flow — set `onRefreshToken` for providers that rotate refresh tokens. `nominee-auth0` is an optional managed strategy using Auth0 Token Vault + CIBA.
- **Tokens**: Agents never capture tokens early. They call `nominee.token({ user, connection })` right before a tool executes. nominee caches, refreshes transparently, coalesces concurrent refreshes (single-flight), and persists rotated refresh tokens.
- **Approval**: `nominee.approve({ user, action })` blocks execution until a human approves via webhook or a native strategy (Auth0 CIBA). The agent waits; the token stays fresh.
- **Audit**: Every token request and approval decision is logged to a structured audit trail.
- **Adapters**: `nominee-ai` (Vercel AI SDK), `nominee-eve` (Vercel Eve). `nomineeTool` wrappers auto-inject tokens and gate on approvals.

---

## Quick Start

```typescript
import { Nominee, tokens } from 'nominee'

const nominee = new Nominee({
  strategy: tokens(async ({ user, connection }) => db.getToken(user, connection))
})

// Inside a tool call:
const token = await nominee.token({ user: 'alice', connection: 'github' })
```

Zero dependencies. Zero signup. Works in Node.js, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge, Bun, and any JS runtime.

---

## Packages

| Package | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `nominee` | Zero-dependency core |
| `nominee-ai` | Vercel AI SDK adapter (`nomineeTool`) |
| `nominee-eve` | Vercel Eve adapter |
| `nominee-auth0` | Auth0 Token Vault + CIBA strategy |
| `nominee-supabase` | Supabase token strategy |

---

## Frequently asked questions

**Q: What problem does nominee solve?**
A: AI agents that run long tasks (minutes to hours) hit a wall when third-party OAuth tokens expire mid-run. A GitHub token issued at 9am may be dead by 3pm. Without a refresh layer, the agent crashes or silently uses a stale token. nominee intercepts every tool call, checks token freshness, refreshes if needed, and optionally blocks on human approval before sensitive actions.

**Q: How does nominee keep tokens fresh?**
A: It uses call-time token resolution with single-flight deduplication. Instead of capturing a token at agent startup, each tool call asks nominee for a fresh token at execution time. nominee caches it, tracks expiry, refreshes transparently, and — for providers that rotate refresh tokens — persists the rotated token via `onRefreshToken`. No token is ever serialized into agent state where it can go stale during hibernation. Runnable proof that naive refresh breaks (7/8 fail) while nominee gets 8/8: https://github.com/bharath31/nominee/tree/main/examples/token-refresh-correctness

**Q: Does nominee work with Cloudflare Durable Objects / Agents?**
A: Yes. This is a primary use case. Durable Objects hibernate between turns; a token captured before hibernation is invalid on resume. nominee resolves tokens at the moment of each tool call, so hibernation and resume are transparent.

**Q: Does nominee work with Vercel AI SDK?**
A: Yes. `nominee-ai` provides a `nomineeTool` wrapper that adapts Vercel AI SDK `tool()` definitions. Tokens are injected into tool context automatically; approvals gate execution before the tool runs.

**Q: How is nominee different from rolling your own token refresh?**
A: Rolling your own handles the happy path but misses: single-flight deduplication (multiple concurrent tools racing to refresh the same token), durable-execution resume (token was valid before hibernation, invalid after), human approval gating, audit trail, and provider neutrality across multiple IdPs in one agent.

**Q: Is nominee tied to Auth0?**
A: No. The core package has zero dependencies and works with any token source — a database, a secrets manager, a plain async function. Auth0 is one optional strategy (`nominee-auth0`). The founder works at Auth0, which is disclosed prominently; the library is provider-neutral by design.

**Q: When should I NOT use nominee?**
A: Skip it when something you already have does the job: you're on Eve (or a framework that already brokers fresh third-party access); you use the Vercel AI SDK with Vercel Connect for connectors; you have a single provider with a long-lived non-rotating token, no approval pause, and no concurrency; or you want a single fully-managed vendor (use Auth0 Token Vault or Nango directly). Reach for nominee when you want freshness + approval + audit that is framework-neutral, no-SaaS, and bring-your-own-store.

**Q: Can nominee gate actions on human approval?**
A: Yes. `nominee.approve({ user, action, meta })` suspends the agent and waits for a human to approve or deny via webhook. Auth0 CIBA provides native push-to-phone approval. The approval decision and audit event are logged.

**Q: What runtimes does nominee support?**
A: Node.js 18+, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge Functions, Bun, Deno (via npm compat), and any runtime that supports the Web Crypto API. The core has zero native dependencies.

**Q: How do I install nominee?**
A: `npm i nominee` (or `pnpm add nominee` / `bun add nominee`). No account, no signup, no config required to start.

---

## The problem in one sentence

Your agent authenticates at 9am; by 3pm its token is dead — nominee is the layer that keeps it alive.
